citations: [Pediatric extremity rhabdomyosarcoma treated with re-brachytherapy in recurrent setting.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35900564/)

evidence.claim:
PubMed ID: 23683937. 2013/04/01 00:00. PURPOSE: Pediatric tumors are generally managed with a multi-modality treatment 
program that includes surgery, chemotherapy, and teletherapy. The use of 
teleth
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 23683937. 2013/04/01 00:00. PURPOSE: Pediatric tumors are generally managed with a multi-modality treatment 
program that includes surgery, chemotherapy, and teletherapy. The use of 
teletherapy in young children can result in significant long-term toxicity 
(especially retardation of growth of bones and organs). The use of brachytherapy 
is an attractive alternative because brachytherapy irradiates small volumes and 
can thus potentially minimize complications.
METHODS AND MATERIALS:
